title: Create Secret
content:
In this example we will save a phrase "This is a really big secret!" as the name `demosecret`. The name can only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters and `-` character. The phrase can be anything.
=== "Kelvin UI" Click on the Secrets menu under Orchestration. Click on the **Add Secret** button in the top right corner. Give your Secret a name which you will reference in your Kelvin SmartApp™ and a value. Then click the **Add** button to save it. You will not see the secret again, so make sure you type it correctly. You can update the value with a new one, but you will not be able to see the original value. You will then see your new secret listed. Congratulations, you now have created a new secret variable. === "Kelvin API" ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/secrets/create" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "name": "demosecret", "value": "This is a really big secret!" }' ``` The response will look like this; ```json title="API cURL Example Response" linenums="1" { "name": "demosecret" } ``` === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username="") client.login(password="") # Create Secret response = client.secret.create_secret(data={ "name": "demosecret", "value": "This is a really big secret!" } ) print(response) ``` You will see the output like this; ```bash title="API Client (Python) Example Response" linenums="1" name='demosecret' created=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 40, 35, 179577, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) created_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' updated=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 40, 35, 179577, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) updated_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' ``` === "Kelvin SDK CLI" ```bash title="Kelvin SDK Command Line Example" linenums="1" kelvin secret create demosecret --value="This is a really big secret!" ``` If everything went well you will get the confirmation response like this; ```bash title="Kelvin SDK Command Line Output" linenums="1" [kelvin.sdk][2025-03-19 20:00:45][I] Creating secret "demosecret" on the platform ```

code_examples.code:
curl -X "POST" \
    "https://<url.kelvin.ai>/api/v4/secrets/create" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer <Your Current Token>" \
    -H "Accept: application/json" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
            "name": "demosecret",
            "value": "This is a really big secret!"
        }'
code_examples.title: API cURL Example

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api.client import Client

    # Login
    client = Client(url="https://<url.kelvin.ai>", username="<your_username>")
    client.login(password="<your_password>")

    # Create Secret
    response = client.secret.create_secret(data={
                                                "name": "demosecret",
                                                "value": "This is a really big secret!"
                                                }
    )

    print(response)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example

code_examples.code: name='demosecret' created=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 40, 35, 179577, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) created_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai' updated=datetime.datetime(2025, 11, 24, 7, 40, 35, 179577, tzinfo=TzInfo(UTC)) updated_by='krn:user:example@kelvin.ai'
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example Response

code_examples.code: kelvin secret create demosecret --value="This is a really big secret!"
code_examples.title: Kelvin SDK Command Line Example
